elements.title: Twisted Tea
elements.contents: ... that Twisted Tea (cans pictured) was rebranded after being sued for trademark infringement?

elements.title: Asma Nadia
elements.contents: ... that Asma Nadia, who has travelled to 72 countries, has published dozens of books – including one with tips for Muslim travellers?

elements.title: reside in Rome
elements.contents: ... that Maxentius was the last Roman emperor in the 4th century to reside in Rome?

elements.title: Thomas Storer
elements.contents: ... that Navajo American mathematician Thomas Storer invented one of the standard notations for compactly and precisely describing string figures?

elements.title: Romanian traditional music
elements.contents: ... that a bride's farewell can be sung in Romanian traditional music as a lament – sometimes also played instrumentally as "the bride's sorrow"?

elements.title: Rin Kurusu
elements.contents: ... that Rin Kurusu did not pursue voice acting earlier because she was already in an idol group?

elements.title: Te Waihorotiu railway station
elements.contents: ... that Te Waihorotiu railway station is named after a now-covered stream that flows beneath Queen Street in Auckland, New Zealand?

elements.title: Roger J. Landry
elements.contents: ... that Roger J. Landry attended both Harvard College and the Pontifical North American College with his identical twin brother?

elements.title: Rhythm Heaven Megamix
elements.contents: ... that a minigame in Rhythm Heaven Megamix allows players to feed turnips to a goat?
